title: Lamborghini stunt in Bengaluru leads police to underworld figure Muthappa Rai’s son
content: A Lamborghini's pre-dawn drifting stunt on Bengaluru's MG Road has led to police scrutiny, with the high-end car traced to Ricky Rai, son of late underworld figure Muthappa Rai. Authorities have seized the vehicle and issued notices to Rai, who has yet to respond. The incident, captured on video, is being investigated for dangerous driving and other violations.

title: 'Drop these players': Gavaskar fumes at IPL no-shows, slams ‘taking for granted’ culture
content: Sunil Gavaskar has criticized overseas players for missing early IPL 2026 matches, citing a pattern of taking franchise hospitality for granted. He urged franchises to adopt stricter policies, warning that late unavailability disrupts team planning and hinders title aspirations.  This stance reflects growing concerns within the cricketing fraternity regarding player commitment.

title: After Trump's warning, Iran threatens to 'completely' close Strait of Hormuz
content: Iran has threatened to completely shut the Strait of Hormuz if the US strikes its energy facilities, warning that regional energy infrastructure hosting US bases would become targets. This escalation follows President Trump's ultimatum.  The conflict has already impacted global oil prices and caused significant damage and displacement on both sides.

title: Mobilize Book Argument? Palantir CTO says AI is a Great Reshorer; jobs and security at stake
content: Shyam Sankar, Palantir's CTO, argues AI will revitalize American manufacturing, not kill jobs, calling it the "Great Reshorer." His book "Mobilize" advocates for AI-powered factories to outpace China, bolstering national security and preventing conflict.  This technological leap aims to restore the "Arsenal of Democracy" and create persistent factory floor jobs.

title: Hardik Pandya told to step aside, ‘let Suryakumar Yadav lead’ at Mumbai Indians
content: Mumbai Indians, despite a star-studded lineup, face a captaincy dilemma ahead of IPL 2026. Former selector Kris Srikkanth suggests Suryakumar Yadav, fresh from leading India to T20 World Cup glory, could take over from Hardik Pandya to revitalize the team's fortunes. Srikkanth highlighted the unusual situation of multiple World Cup-winning captains playing under Pandya.

title: CCS recognises 'significant impact' of West Asia conflict
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi's cabinet committee on security discussed measures to diversify import sources for key sectors like chemicals and pharmaceuticals due to the West Asia conflict's global economic impact. The government is preparing short-, medium-, and long-term strategies to safeguard citizens and ensure the availability of essential goods like fertilizers and coal.

title: ‘Dhurandhar: The Revenge’ box office collection day 4: Film inches close to Rs 700 crore
content: Ranveer Singh's 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge' has stormed the box office, raking in Rs 500 crore domestically and Rs 690 crore worldwide. Directed by Aditya Dhar, this spy thriller, a sequel to 'Dhurandhar,' captivated audiences with its gripping narrative and action. The film's impressive opening weekend and subsequent strong performance are setting new benchmarks for Bollywood.

title: Abhishek Sharma had ‘six-hitting skills from the start’, teammate reveals untold story
content: Abhishek Sharma faced early struggles in the T20 World Cup but bounced back to help India win. His friend Nehal Wadhera shared insights into Abhishek's talent and hard work. Wadhera also discussed his own IPL journey, learning from coaches like Ricky Ponting and playing under captains Rohit Sharma and Shreyas Iyer. Punjab Kings aims for the title in IPL 2026.

title: Tesla finance VP Sendil Palani exits, recalls Tesla Deathwatch' days
content: After seventeen years, Tesla's senior finance member Sendil Palani has departed, having joined when the company was on the brink of collapse in late 2008. Palani lauded his finance team for making complex tasks appear effortless and credited cross-functional late-night sessions for invaluable real-world learning. He thanked CEO Elon Musk for championing first-principles thinking.

title: Petronet LNG, HDFC Bank & more: Top stocks to watch today
content: Nomura cut Petronet LNG's target price due to West Asia crisis impacting volumes, while HSBC maintained a hold on Blue Star, citing demanding valuations. BofA Securities remains constructive on HDFC Bank despite chairman's exit, and Citigroup upgraded Dr Lal Pathlabs, expecting strong revenue growth and attractive valuations.

title: Tankers with US LPG & Russian oil arrive at New Mangalore Port
content: Two vessels, the LPG tanker Pyxis Pioneer from the US and the Russian crude oil tanker Aqua Titan, arrived at New Mangalore Port on Sunday. The port, which boasts India's largest underground LPG storage, waived cargo charges for these shipments. The Aqua Titan's course change from China to India remained unclear.

title: Candace Owens speaks out after Joe Rogan call as video resurfaces
content: Candace Owens supported Joe Rogan after his comments about Erika Kirk resurfaced online. She said Rogan speaks what people already think, which is why he stays popular. The issue started from an old video where Kirk discussed serious topics. Now, the debate has shifted to free speech, public opinion, and whether such comments go too far.

title: Jannik Sinner faces drama in Miami Open win after fan disruption
content: Jannik Sinner won his Miami Open 2026 second-round match but faced an unexpected moment when a loud fan disrupted play. Damir Dzumhur complained, and the umpire called security to handle the situation. After the match, Sinner apologized for the incident. He also matched Novak Djokovic’s record of 24 straight Masters 1000 sets and now moves to the third round.

title: Hailee Steinfeld and Josh Allen’s Malibu move sparks baby No. 2 buzz
content: Hailee Steinfeld and Josh Allen are reportedly planning to buy a large Malibu home as they prepare for their growing family. With a baby on the way, the couple is thinking long term, including life after Allen’s NFL career. Sources say they want a spacious West Coast base that fits their rising profiles and future plans together.

title: If America strikes us, we should attack Indian cities like Delhi and Mumbai: Ex-Pak envoy
content: Former Pakistani envoy Abdul Basit stated that striking Indian cities like New Delhi and Mumbai would be Islamabad's "default move" if Pakistan were attacked by the US. He asserted that Pakistan's nuclear deterrence is India-specific, not aimed at the US, and that any aggression would be met with retaliation against India.

title: Air India to deploy more pilots for Dreamliners on West route
content: Air India is reinforcing its Boeing 787 Dreamliner fleet with pilots transitioning from Boeing 777s and narrow-body aircraft due to increasing airspace restrictions. This move aims to manage longer flight durations on Europe and UK routes, as pilots express fatigue from extended duty times and the need for dispensations from flight duty limitations.

title: PBKS playing XI for IPL 2026: Shreyas Iyer to lead, who will fill Ferguson’s void?
content: Punjab Kings reached the IPL 2025 final under Shreyas Iyer, marking a significant turnaround. For IPL 2026, they've bolstered their squad with Australian all-rounders Ben Dwarshuis and Cooper Connolly, aiming to strengthen their bowling and middle-order for another title challenge.

title: UAPA misuse not a model for Viksit Bharat: SC judge
content: SC judge Ujjal Bhuyan emphasized that a developed India by 2047 requires greater tolerance for debate and dissent, without criminalizing them. He highlighted that thousands arrested under the UAPA with minuscule convictions indicate overuse or misuse of the law, hindering justice delivery and the ideal of a developed nation.

title: PM Modi is India's longest serving head of govt
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has become the longest-serving head of government, surpassing the record previously held by Pawan Kumar Chamling. This milestone marks 25 continuous years in leadership, beginning with his tenure as Gujarat Chief Minister in October 2001 and continuing as Prime Minister since May 2014.

title: Bengal polls: 8.6% of voters to know fate from today; 1st supplementary list to decide electoral right of 27.2 lakh
content: Over 27 lakh people in Bengal will learn their voting eligibility on Monday as the Election Commission releases the first supplementary voters' list. These individuals were placed in an 'under adjudication' category, with their fate hinging on document scrutiny by judicial officers. Those who fail to clear the hurdle can appeal the decision.

title: Back in Ladakh to rousing reception, Wangchuk calls for ‘give and take’ solution
content: Released activist Sonam Wangchuk urged the Union government for "win-win" talks with Ladakhi leaders, emphasizing a "give and take" approach.  He vowed to continue his struggle if necessary, highlighting his fight for Ladakh's culture and environment. Wangchuk expressed hope for justice regarding the September 24 firing and the release of arrested individuals.

title: Few women get past collegium's subjective assessment: SC judge
content: Supreme Court Justice Ujjal Bhuyan highlighted a stark gender disparity in higher judicial appointments, noting women excel in lower judicial services but are underrepresented in High Courts and the Supreme Court due to subjective collegium criteria. He stressed the need for greater gender parity for India to achieve developed nation status by 2047.

title: AI can never perform core job of judgment writing: SC judge
content: Supreme Court's Justice Vikram Nath asserted AI can aid judicial processes but won't replace judges' core judgment-writing role.  He highlighted AI's limitations in complex cases like matrimonial disputes and constitutional matters. Other judges echoed concerns about AI's potential for bias and the need for human conscience in justice delivery, emphasizing its role as a facilitator, not a substitute.

title: Maoist endgame nears, but no political fanfare in offing
content: The March 31 deadline for eliminating Left-wing extremism may pass without fanfare, having successfully created a psychological impetus for stakeholders. This pressure led to the neutralization or surrender of most CPI(Maoist) leadership, fracturing the group and pushing remaining members towards surrender.

title: Kilometrage allowance hiked by 25% for train crew
content: Indian Railways has boosted Kilometrage Allowance and Allowance in lieu of Kilometrage by 25% for over two lakh running staff, effective January 2024. This decision aligns compensation with the 50% Dearness Allowance hike, addressing long-standing demands for better financial benefits for essential operational personnel.

title: CAFE 3.0 rollout: Penalty recovery for carmakers remains a grey area
content: Government seeks clarity on recovering hefty penalties from carmakers for missing fuel efficiency targets. With stricter norms ahead, the PMO is pushing ministries to define who evaluates, approves, and recovers the dues. Over Rs 8,800 crore in penalties have been levied for past non-compliance, yet no recovery has occurred.

title: IPL 2026: CSK induct Suresh Raina, Matthew Hayden into first-ever Hall of Fame
content: Chennai Super Kings inducted Suresh Raina and Matthew Hayden into their inaugural Hall of Fame at the "Roar’26" fan event. Raina, 'Chinna Thala', was instrumental in four title wins and remains CSK's leading run-scorer. Hayden contributed to the 2010 IPL victory and was the first CSK player to win the Orange Cap.

title: 'Pop a pill and carry on': The workplace silence on India's menstrual leave debate
content: Millions of women navigate monthly hormonal cycles impacting energy and resilience, yet modern workplaces expect consistent performance. The debate around mandatory menstrual leave in India highlights concerns about potential hiring bias versus the need for biological accommodation. Experts and individuals offer varied perspectives.

title: Koneru Humpy withdraws from Women’s Candidates, cites safety concerns
content: Top Indian Grandmaster Koneru Humpy has withdrawn from the FIDE Women's Candidates Tournament, prioritizing her personal safety and well-being. Citing security concerns despite assurances, Humpy's decision opens a spot for Anna Muzychuk. While India retains representation with R Vaishali and Divya Deshmukh, Humpy's withdrawal raises questions about potential FIDE penalties and impacts Indian chess.

title: Watch: Iran claims it struck 'enemy' F-15 fighter jet intercepted near Hormuz Island
content: Iran's air defense claims to have intercepted and struck an enemy F-15 fighter jet near Hormuz Island, firing ground-to-air missiles. This incident follows a recent report of a US F-35 stealth fighter being damaged over Iran and missile launches towards Diego Garcia, escalating regional military tensions.

title: Pathan explains ‘identification of talent’ as reason why DC, PBKS haven’t won IPL yet
content: Former India all-rounder Irfan Pathan believes Delhi Capitals' past struggles with talent identification, citing instances like letting go of AB de Villiers and Andre Russell, contributed to their title drought. In contrast, Punjab Kings' recent progress, marked by strong leadership, strategic auction buys like Shreyas Iyer, and improved squad depth, has propelled them towards success, reaching the IPL final.

title: Will Trump's 48-hour ultimatum to Iran leave him red-faced again?
content: As a 48-hour deadline looms, Iran has retaliated against President Trump's threat to "obliterate" its power plants. Tehran warns that energy facilities in countries hosting US bases are now "lawful targets." The escalating conflict, now in its fourth week, has already seen cross-border attacks and rising casualties, with oil prices surging.

title: The 50 Winner: Shiv Thakare lifts the trophy; takes home Rs 50 lakh for a lucky fan
content: Shiv Thakare has clinched the inaugural season of 'The 50', triumphing over formidable competitors like Mr Faisu and Rajat Dalal. His victory, marked by a Rs 50 lakh prize for a fan, underscores his enduring popularity.

title: Why Kerala's 'semi-final' verdict has put Left on red alert
content: Local body poll results in Kerala signal a potential shift, with the Congress-led UDF gaining momentum against the ruling LDF. The UDF secured key urban strongholds, raising concerns about anti-incumbency for Chief Minister Pinarayi Vijayan's government. The BJP also shows strategic expansion, hinting at a more competitive assembly election.

title: Dhurandhar 2 actor Mustafa Ahmed, once a Rs 10,000 gym trainer
content: Mustafa Ahmed, once a struggling gym trainer earning Rs 10,000 monthly, defied dyslexia and societal expectations to become a sought-after fitness expert. His journey, marked by a pivotal moment with a client, led him to train Bollywood stars like Hrithik Roshan and eventually to an acting role in 'Dhurandhar 2', proving that passion and perseverance can overcome any obstacle.

title: As INDIA bloc blocks Congress in states, why grand old party should quit alliance
content: The INDIA bloc faces internal strife post-Rajya Sabha elections, with allies like SP, CPM, and CPI(ML) criticising Congress. Cross-voting and state-level rivalries, particularly in Maharashtra, Delhi, West Bengal, Tamil Nadu, and Kerala, have frayed the alliance. Regional parties question Congress's leadership, suggesting a need for independent rebuilding before any grand coalition.

title: Iran's missile map decoded: Who falls within its expanding strike range
content: Iran's extensive missile program, ranging from short-range tactical systems to long-range weapons covering much of the Middle East, serves as a core element of its deterrence and retaliation strategy. With capabilities reaching up to 2,500km, Tehran can target regional adversaries and US bases, projecting power and ensuring a retaliatory capacity.

title: IPL 2026: Ishan Kishan gets animated send-off as things heat up in SRH camp
content: Sunrisers Hyderabad's stand-in captain Ishan Kishan was playfully sent off by teammate Zeeshan Ansari during a practice game. Kishan, who is leading SRH in Pat Cummins' absence due to injury, responded sportingly to the leg-spinner's animated gesture. This incident occurs as Kishan takes on leadership duties following his stellar T20 World Cup performance.

title: 'Europe within Iranian missiles range': IDF warns after Diego Garcia strike; UK says 'no evidence'
content: Iranian forces launched long-range missiles for the first time, striking near Israel's nuclear site and injuring dozens. The missiles, capable of reaching European capitals, were fired at the US-UK base Diego Garcia. These actions escalate the conflict, with Iran stating they targeted military installations in response to US-Israeli attacks.

title: Iowa takes down defending champs UF in thriller
content: Alvaro Folgueiras nailed a 3-pointer with 4.5 seconds remaining, and Iowa eliminated defending national champion Florida in the second round of the men's NCAA tournament.

title: Darling calls own shot to keep St. John's dancing
content: Dylan Darling's layup as time expired sent St. John's to its first Sweet 16 since 1999 with a 67-65 win over Kansas in the men's NCAA tournament.

title: Wrong 'answer' as Kentucky hit with historic loss
content: On Sunday, there was one stat that couldn't be easily dismissed -- the margin of defeat. The Wildcats' 19-point loss in an 82-63 defeat against Iowa State was Kentucky's largest defeat in an NCAA tournament game since 1972.

title: Booker dominates Oregon with 40 in Texas win
content: Madison Booker scored a career-high 40 points in a dazzling display of shotmaking and No. 1 Texas rolled past No. 8 Oregon 100-58.

title: Battle sends Minn. to first Sweet 16 in 21 years
content: Amaya Battle hit a tiebreaking jump shot with 0.7 seconds left, lifting Minnesota past Mississippi 65-63 in the second round Sunday to send the Gophers to the Sweet 16 for the first time in 21 years.

title: Kansas' Self: No decision yet on coaching future
content: Longtime Kansas coach Bill Self said after Sunday's season-ending loss to St. John's that he has not yet made a decision about his coaching future, later adding that any decision will be based solely on his health.

title: Luka avoids suspension after 16th tech rescinded
content: The NBA has rescinded Luka Doncic's 16th technical foul, allowing him to play in the Los Angeles Lakers' road game against the Detroit Pistons on Monday night.

title: Hockey world mourns loss of Wild reporter Pierce
content: Jessi Pierce, who covered the Wild as the correspondent for NHL.com for the past decade. and her three children were killed Saturday in a house fire in Minnesota.

title: 👀 Soccer star Jorginho upset with Chappell Roan
content: Pop singer Chappell Roan has said she "does not hate children" in response to former Chelsea and Arsenal player Jorginho's criticism.

title: Women's March Madness Day 3 takeaways: How every team won
content: We're breaking down all of Sunday's action and sharing how each winner can advance to the Elite Eight.

title: No plan, no fight: Spurs hurtling toward relegation after limp loss to Forest
content: The thousand-yard stare has become the only thing you can guarantee at the Tottenham Hotspur Stadium, and it is staring into the abyss of relegation.

title: Man City Carabao Cup win hints Premier League title race not over
content: Arsenal hoped to win the first of four trophies. Instead, Man City show why they cannot be overlooked.

title: Hyo Joo Kim edges Nelly Korda for wire-to-wire Founders win
content: Hyo Joo Kim held off Nelly Korda on Sunday at Sharon Heights to complete a wire-to-wire victory in the Fortinet Founders Cup.

title: Rare air: Tyler Reddick in elite club with Darlington win
content: The "Track Too Tough To Tame" tested Tyler Reddick in every way possible Sunday, and this year's top star in NASCAR naturally passed with flying colors for his fourth victory of the season.

title: 'They're not even close': Why a Giannis trade might be even more complicated this summer
content: As the Giannis trade watch enters another offseason, a rare ownership structure and financial turmoil have left opposing teams wondering: Who's in charge here?

title: NCAA tournament: Matchups, capsules for every team
content: The road to the Frozen Four is set. Here's a look at the complete schedule, plus key information on all 16 teams.

title: Kentucky's Quaintance uses manga comics to fuel recovery
content: The Kentucky forward has gotten into the hobby as a way to help time pass while he heals from injury.

title: Men's tournament odds: Michigan leapfrogs Arizona as title favorite, Purdue improves
content: Teams' odds to advance and win the men's college basketball tournament entering second day of second round.

title: Women's tournament: UConn remains favorite as UCLA's odds improve
content: Odds for each team to advance through the bracket and to win the 2026 women's college basketball championship.

title: Potential busts to avoid in 2026
content: Who does Tristan H. Cockcroft think will underachieve in 2026? We have the names to steer clear of when drafting your rosters.

title: Risers and fallers: Doncic stars, but is it time to drop Giannis?
content: Luka Doncic is on another planet, and Ayo Dosunmu and Daniss Jenkins are on the rise, but should fantasy basketball managers keep Giannis Antetokounmpo and Mikal Bridges rostered?

title: Bryson DeChambeau wins again, beats Jon Rahm in playoff
content: Bryson DeChambeau won for the second straight week by saving par on the final hole for a 6-under 65 and blistering a 3-wood from a wet lie in the rough on the par-5 18th in a playoff to set up birdie and defeat Jon Rahm at LIV Golf South Africa on Sunday.

title: Transfer rumors, news: Arsenal step up pursuit of Roma star to replace Ben White
content: Arsenal are on a quest to replace Ben White and have landed on an in-form option from AS Roma. Transfer Talk has the latest.

title: Govt pushes for PNG connections, asks all ministries to assess demand
content: the centre has asked all ministries to assess potential demand of piped natural gas . it has also urged govt establishments, residential colonies and canteens to switch to PNG . push for PNG is also aimed at reducing dependence on imported LPG in the long term .

title: West Bengal Assembly polls 2026: AIMIM to ally to Humayun Kabir’s AJUP
content: Humayun Kabir’s AJUP has announced candidates for 149 Assembly seats including on high profile seats like Bhabanipur and Nandigram

title: Battle for Bhabanipur: Mamata begins campaign with workers’ meet
content: In the 2026 Assembly polls, the contest has become interesting because BJP leader Suvendu Adhikari is contesting against the Chief Minister. The Assembly has seen about 47,000 electors deleted during SIR and fate of 14,000 others hanging under judicial adjudication

title: Karnataka bypolls: Fear of rebellion looms large over Congress in Davangere
content: Though KPCC president and Deputy Chief Minister D.K. Shivakumar claimed in Bengaluru that candidates’ selection for the Assembly bypoll was based on consensus and the decision was unanimous, a Congress leader and disappointed ticket aspirant for Davangere South constituency Mohammad Sadiq Pehelwan announced in Davangere that he would challenge Congress candidate Samarth as a rebel candidate

title: ‘Vidiyal Payanam’ initiative would improve during DMK 2.0 government: Tamil Nadu CM Stalin
content: In a social media post, Mr. Stalin said 897.82 crore free bus trips were undertaken on 7,712 buses till March 1 this year

title: Wangchuk returns to Ladakh after NSA revocation, calls for relief to detainees
content: He was released recently by the Centre after nearly six months in detention under the National Security Act.

title: BJD ready for legal battle to ensure 6 suspended MLAs' disqualification from Odisha Assembly
content: The suspended legislators are Chakramani Kanhar, Naba Kishor Mallick, Souvic Biswal, Subasini Jena, Ramakanta Bhoi and Devi Ranjan Tripathy

title: Opposition seeks arrest of AAP leader in Punjab
content: Congress, BJP, and SAD demand arrest of former AAP minister after warehousing corporation official’s death

title: Opposition MPs, activists oppose amendments to Transgender Persons Act
content: Public hearing in Delhi raises concerns over proposed changes to Transgender Persons (Protection of Rights) Act, including removal of self-perceived gender identity provision

title: Defeat of BJP must to save Assam, says citizen forum
content: The Assam Nagorik Sanmilani, which urged Raijor Dal on to join the Congress-led bloc a few days ago, says the BJP’s divisive politics have hollowed out the Assamese identity

title: Assam police camp attacked by ULFA(I), four injured
content: The assailant fired indiscriminately and lobbed several grenades at the commando camp in eastern Assam’s Tinsukia district

title: T.N. Assembly election: residents of Tiruttani constituency seek more support to improve their livelihood
content: Farmers, who recently set up a producer organisation and started growing millets, complain that they still do not have machinery needed to process the crop. Residents say work to lay a second access road to Subramaniaswami Temple is yet to be completed, with land acquisition still under way

title: BJP will draw a blank this time too, says Pinarayi Vijayan
content: The Kerala Chief Minister doesn’t see defections from the Left helping either the BJP or the Congress, which, according to him, is the BJP’s ‘B team’; for the State to be in step with the modern era, the Left should continue to be in govt, he argues

title: IRCTC penalises caterer for not serving tea on Duronto Express
content: ₹5,000 penalty imposed after one of the passengers lodged a complaint that the hot beverage was not provided

title: Forest Department seizes 500 kg red sanders near Gudiyatham
content: In an attempt to flee, the car sped and slipped into an open land at an isolated spot near Gudiyatham town

title: Kashmiris collect donations for war-hit Iran; Iranian Embassy says 'will never forget kindness'
content: The donations have been collected, especially in Budgam and Baramulla, with a considerable population of Shias.

title: Khandre honoured in Bidar after elevation as national president of Veerashaiva Lingayat Mahasabha
content: Speaking at the function, Siddharoodha Mutt seer Shivakumar Swamiji described Mr. Khandre as a leader shaped by his virtues, combining compassion, courage and commitment to public service

title: Commercial LPG quota in Maharashtra raised by 20% on Centre’s directive
content: Priority for the additional supply will be given to hotels, dhabas, restaurants, industrial canteens, food processing industries, and the dairy sector

title: Jana Sena party aims for one lakh memberships in Srikakulam
content: Villagers were happy with the speedy development of rural areas and were coming forward to enrol their names in ‘Udyami’ membership drive, Mr. Ravikumar said

title: Welfare of handloom artisans priority for government, says Minister
content: Savitha says government is implementing several schemes to strengthen the handloom sector; subsidies on raw materials, net funds, and training in modern designs being provided and up to 90% subsidy is being offered on purchase of equipment to enhance productivity

title: Judiciary must neither be eternal critic nor cheerleader: Justice Bhuyan
content: The judge flagged the ‘reckless’ registration of criminal cases, even for student protests, memes, and social media posts, speaking at the 1st Supreme Court Bar Association National Conference

title: Assembly Elections highlights: Congress releases final list of 7 candidates for Assam Assembly polls
content: The Congress and DMK broadly agreed to share 15 seats each in the 30-member Puducherry Assembly, which goes to polls on April 9

title: NCP(SP) ‘unites’ behind Saseendran after Elathur candidate row
content: Party national working president P.C. Chacko admits there were differences over candidate selection in Elathur

title: Unable to find space for poll office owing to threats, alleges Kunhikrishnan
content: Former CPI(M) leader and rebel candidate says he was recently forced to dismantle an election office after the property owner was allegedly threatened

title: Election officials seize stainless steel utensils, saris, unaccounted cash in Vellore, nearby districts
content: The vehicle-checks follow the enforcement of Model Code of Conduct for the Tamil Nadu Assembly elections

title: West Asia conflict: PM Modi reviews West Asia situation, underlines diversification imperatives
content: The meeting took stock of the current global situation in the wake of the West Asia conflict and the measures taken to protect consumer and industry interests, which is the key focus of the government, the sources said.

title: Brahmin faces of Naam Tamilar Katchi in electoral fray, a shift from the past
content: Faced with stereotypes in DMK and Periyarite political circles, Seeman’s NTK has become a refuge for Brahmins who want to work for Tamil nationalism in TN

title: Jeff Bezos' ex-wife makes another 'record-setting gift
content: The TOI Tech Desk is a dedicated team of journalists committed to delivering the latest and most relevant news from the world of technology to readers of The Times of India. TOI Tech Desk’s news coverage spans a wide spectrum across gadget launches, gadget reviews, trends, in-depth analysis, exclusive reports and breaking stories that impact technology and the digital universe. Be it how-tos or the latest happenings in AI, cybersecurity, personal gadgets, platforms like WhatsApp, Instagram, Facebook and more; TOI Tech Desk brings the news with accuracy and authenticity. title: IAF staffer held in Assam for leaking defence info to Pakistan
content: the accused, Sumit Kumar, 36, was working as Multi-Tasking Staff at air force station . he allegedly passed sensitive military information to Pakistan-based handlers . investigators said he had been in contact with Pakistani intelligence operatives since 2023 and was sharing confidential information for money .

title: With 7th regiment, Army expands Pinaka fleet
content: the Indian Army has seven Pinaka rocket launcher regiments deployed along its borders with china and pakistan . the eighth regiment has received more than half its equipment . it should become operational before the end of 2026, a source says .

title: Iran war not to end soon? Treasury's Bessent says US has 'plenty of money to fund this war'
content: At TOI World Desk, our dedicated team of seasoned journalists and passionate writers tirelessly sifts through the vast tapestry of global events to bring you the latest news and diverse perspectives round the clock. With an unwavering commitment to accuracy, depth, and timeliness, we strive to keep you informed about the ever-evolving world, delivering a nuanced understanding of international affairs to our readers. Join us on a journey across continents as we unravel the stories that shape our interconnected world. Read More

title: No AI, No FPI? Why Foreign Investors Are Pulling Out Of Indian Markets
content: According to market observers, most Indian companies lack a significant AI value chain, prompting foreign investors to look at greener pastures.

title: $2 Million Toll On Ships To Pass Strait Of Hormuz? What Iran Said
content: Iran's President Masoud Pezeshkian has said the Strait of Hormuz is "open to everyone" except Iran's adversaries as he set out Tehran's policy on X.

title: US-Israel-Iran War Updates:Indian National Injured As Debris Falls In Abu Dhabi's Al Shawamekh
content: US President Donald Trump threatened to "obliterate" Iran's power plants if Tehran does not reopen the Strait of Hormuz for shipping traffic.

title: 'Indirect Yet Palpable': Iran War's Impact On Real Estate In Mumbai
content: Iran War Impact: Mumbai's real estate sector has started witnessing a drop in demand from global companies for office spaces.

title: 72-Year-Old Woman Killed In Fire At High-Rise In Mumbai's Goregaon: Cops
content: A 72-year-old woman died after a fire broke out in a 24-storey residential building in Mumbai's Goregaon East area on Sunday evening, officials said.

title: Assam BJP Leader Nandita Garlosa Joins Congress After Not Getting Ticket
content: Former minister in the Assam government, Nandita Garlosa, has joined the Congress ahead of the assembly election

title: RSS Backs Right To Faith, Rejects Religious Discrimination
content: As part of the centenary year celebrations of the RSS, the organisation's Meghalaya unit organised a Prominent Citizens' Meet in Shillong to facilitate dialogue and exchange of ideas among distinguished members of the society.

title: Mamata Banerjee Holds Election Strategy Meeting With Trinamool Workers
content: Abhishek Banerjee said there is a need for repeated voter outreach and asked local leaders not to rely on instructions from above, but to engage directly with constituents

title: Citizens Must Be Safeguarded From Iran War Effect: PM Modi At Key Meet
content: Defence Minister Rajnath Singh, Home Minister Amit Shah, Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman and External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar are members of the CCS, which is headed by PM Modi.

title: Air Force Staffer Arrested For Spying For Pak Handlers In Assam
content: A civilian employee posted at an Indian Air Force station in Assam has been arrested for allegedly spying and sharing sensitive information with Pakistani handlers, Rajasthan Intelligence said on Sunday.

title: US, Iran Trade Threats To "Destroy" Middle East Infrastructure
content: The tit-for-tat threats came as the war entered its fourth week and continued to reverberate across the Middle East, with alarm mounting over strikes around nuclear sites.

title: Old, Ugly 'Flying Tank' US Wanted To Scrap Shines In Hormuz Missions
content: The A-10 'Warthog' earned its reputation not in the air, but close to the ground, in the chaos of close air support (CAS) where troops in contact with the enemy needed urgent help

title: 'We Have Plenty Of Money To Fund Iran War': US Treasury Chief
content: The US military's request for $200 billion in additional funding for the Iran war faces stiff opposition in Congress, with Democrats and even some Republicans questioning the need after large defense appropriations last year.

title: Tutor Kills Girl, 14, After Family Objects To His Advances In Odisha: Cops
content: A private tutor was arrested in Odisha's Mayurbhanj district for allegedly killing his 14-year-old student, whose family warned him after getting to know about his advances, police said on Sunday.

title: Iran Says Will Completely Close Strait Of Hormuz If US Targets Power Plants
content: The military said it would also strike Israel's "power plants, energy, and information and communications technology (ICT) infrastructure".

title: CIA Ex Chief Who Hunted Bin Laden Has A Hormuz Advice For Trump
content: Leon Panetta, who ran the Central Intelligence Agency (CIA) and the Pentagon under former president Barack Obama, has singled out US President Donald Trump as the only man responsible for the current situation

title: CIA, Mossad Track Every Signal For Signs Of Iran's "Unseen" Supreme Leader
content: Intelligence agencies around the world, including the CIA and the Mossad, waited on Friday to see if Mojtaba would follow his father's tradition and give a New Year's address.

title: Sasikala-Ramadoss Alliance May Incur Heavy Cost On NDA In Tamil Nadu Polls
content: The alliance is positioning itself as a force meant to split votes and inflict damage on the AIADMK-led NDA

title: Lashkar Terrorist Bilal Arif Sarafi Stabbed, Shot Dead By Family In Pakistan
content: The exact reason behind the murder is not known yet, but intelligence sources suspect a family dispute.

title: Men's March Madness Sunday takeaways: How Iowa, St. John's, Tennessee, more won
content: alvaro Folgueiras sinks a go-ahead 3-pointer in the final seconds as Iowa beats the defending champions . the Hawkeyes controlled most of the game, leading for 66% of it, thanks in large part to their ability to match the Gators physicality .

title: Organisational changes are being made to reach every part of India, says Sunil Ambekar of RSS
content: “The core idea has always been that this is a Hindu nation, where Hindutva is understood as a way of life. This way of life is not opposed to anyone, nor is it meant to eliminate or harm others,” says Sunil Ambekar

title: Hyderabad paediatrician who pushed ORS labelling reforms served legal notice by pharma firms
content: The notice states that the companies are science-based and compliant with the Food Safety and Standards Act, 2006, and asserts that their claims are backed by scientific evidence and regulatory approvals

title: Even Muslim-majority countries don’t allow namaz on roads, says Sunil Ambekar of RSS
content: Mr. Ambekar, in charge of the RSS’s national publicity,  responds to questions on how incidents in BJP-ruled States — such as FIRs over namaz on roads, iftar on a boat in the Ganga, or the Char Dham temples asking for ‘Sanatani’ affidavits — impact the RSS’s message of unity in diversity.

title: Trinamool fields Assam MLA Sherman Ali Ahmed for Mandia seat
content: Controversial MLA, who once proposed a Miya Museum at Srimanta Sankaradeva Kalakshetra in Guwahati, gets Trinamool Congress ticket for Assam Assembly poll

title: BJP says Congress gave away Ramzan kits in Davangere in violation of poll code
content: The food kits containing rice, vermicelli, lentils, sugar and cooking oil packets had the names of Congress leaders, according to BJP leaders

title: Three-day national conference on floriculture in Shivamogga to begin on March 23
content: Karnataka is a hub of commercial flower cultivation, with 30% of the country’s total flower production, says B. Hemla Naik, director of education of the university

title: Men's March Madness live tracker: Updates from all of Sunday's games
content: the first half of the Sweet 16 is set, with 16 teams playing for one of the final eight tickets to the second weekend of the 2026 men's NCAA tournament . the second round continues on sunday, with a record of 15.1 points and a better shot than 70% fro . bidunga has struggled in other big games, five points and four points in two games against Houston, two points against Arizona and eight points against North Carolina .

title: Women's March Madness live updates: Previews, action from Sunday's second round
content: the second round of the Madness gets going Sunday with 16 teams vying to make it to the Sweet 16 . a key to winning the game for every team is coming back . the ducks have won three of their past four games, their best streak since winning four in a row .

title: Jets paying combined $48M for Rodgers, Fields to not be their QB in 2026
